title: Incident Response Plan
verbatim: Financial institutions shall develop, maintain, and test an incident response plan (IRP) that addresses the identification, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activities for cybersecurity incidents. The IRP shall be reviewed at least annually and after every major incident.
translation: You must have a written plan covering exactly what to do when a cyber incident happens — how to detect it, contain it, remove the threat, recover systems, and review what happened. The plan must be tested, reviewed every year, and updated after any significant incident.

artifacts.name: Incident Response Plan
artifacts.description: Comprehensive IRP covering identification, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activities

artifacts.name: IRP Testing Records
artifacts.description: Records of IRP testing through tabletop exercises or simulations

artifacts.name: Post-Incident Review Reports
artifacts.description: Reports from post-incident reviews with lessons learned and improvements

title: Cybersecurity Strategy
verbatim: Financial institutions shall develop a cybersecurity strategy that is aligned with the institution's business strategy and risk appetite. The strategy shall define the institution's cybersecurity vision, objectives, and roadmap for strengthening cyber resilience. The strategy shall be approved by the board and reviewed at least annually.
translation: You need a board-approved cybersecurity strategy that aligns with your business goals and risk appetite. It should lay out your cybersecurity vision, specific objectives, and a roadmap for improving cyber resilience over time. Review it at least yearly.

artifacts.name: Cybersecurity Strategy
artifacts.description: Board-approved cybersecurity strategy with vision, objectives, and roadmap

artifacts.name: Board Strategy Approval
artifacts.description: Board approval records for the cybersecurity strategy

artifacts.name: Strategy Review Report
artifacts.description: Annual review report on cybersecurity strategy effectiveness and updates

title: Cybersecurity Governance
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a cybersecurity governance structure that clearly defines roles, responsibilities, and accountability for cybersecurity across the three lines of defence. The governance structure shall ensure adequate segregation of duties between cybersecurity operations, risk management, and audit functions.
translation: Your cybersecurity governance must have clear roles and accountability across all three lines of defence. Ensure proper segregation between people who run security operations, those who manage risk, and those who audit — they should not be the same people.
evidence: Three lines of defence model documentation

artifacts.name: Cybersecurity Governance Framework
artifacts.description: Framework defining cybersecurity governance across three lines of defence

artifacts.name: Cybersecurity RACI Matrix
artifacts.description: RACI matrix defining cybersecurity roles and responsibilities

artifacts.name: Segregation of Duties Evidence
artifacts.description: Evidence of segregation between security operations, risk, and audit

title: Incident Detection and Reporting
verbatim: Financial institutions shall implement mechanisms to detect, analyse, and report cybersecurity incidents. Material incidents shall be reported to Bank Negara Malaysia within four hours of detection. Financial institutions shall maintain a documented incident classification framework to determine materiality.
translation: You need technical tools and processes to detect security incidents quickly. Any significant (material) incident must be reported to BNM within 4 hours of detection — not 4 hours after confirmation. You also need a written classification framework so staff know what counts as material.

artifacts.name: Incident Classification Framework
artifacts.description: Framework for classifying incidents and determining materiality for BNM reporting

artifacts.name: BNM Incident Reporting Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure for reporting material incidents to BNM within four hours

artifacts.name: Detection Tool Configuration
artifacts.description: Configuration evidence for SIEM and incident detection mechanisms

artifacts.name: Sample Incident Notification
artifacts.description: Sample incident report or BNM notification record

title: Cyber Crisis Management
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a cyber crisis management plan that defines the governance, escalation, and decision-making processes during a significant cybersecurity event. The plan shall include roles for executive management, legal, communications, and technology teams. Crisis simulations shall be conducted at least annually.
translation: You need a cyber crisis management plan for major incidents. It must define who makes decisions, how escalation works, and the roles of executive management, legal, communications, and technical teams. Run crisis simulations at least once a year.
evidence: Crisis team roles and contact information

artifacts.name: Cyber Crisis Management Plan
artifacts.description: Plan defining governance, escalation, and decision-making during cyber crises

artifacts.name: Crisis Simulation Records
artifacts.description: Records of annual cyber crisis simulations and outcomes

artifacts.name: Crisis Team Roster
artifacts.description: Documented crisis team roles, responsibilities, and contact information

title: SOC Operations
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish or procure a security operations centre (SOC) capable of providing twenty-four by seven monitoring of the institution's technology environment. The SOC shall be staffed with qualified security analysts and shall operate documented processes for alert triage, investigation, and escalation.
translation: You must have a SOC providing 24/7 monitoring of your technology environment. It needs qualified security analysts and documented processes for triaging, investigating, and escalating alerts. This can be in-house or outsourced.

artifacts.name: SOC Operational Procedures
artifacts.description: Documented procedures for SOC operations, alert triage, and escalation

artifacts.name: SOC Staffing Evidence
artifacts.description: 24/7 staffing schedule and analyst qualification records

artifacts.name: SOC Performance Reports
artifacts.description: Regular reports on SOC performance metrics and effectiveness

title: SIEM Implementation
verbatim: Financial institutions shall implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system to collect, correlate, and analyse security events from all critical systems. SIEM rules and use cases shall be regularly tuned and updated to address evolving threats. Log sources shall be validated for completeness.
translation: Deploy a SIEM system that collects and correlates security logs from all critical systems. Regularly tune the detection rules and use cases to keep up with evolving threats. Make sure all critical log sources are actually feeding into the SIEM.
evidence: Log source inventory and coverage analysis

artifacts.name: SIEM Architecture Documentation
artifacts.description: Architecture and deployment documentation for the SIEM system

artifacts.name: Log Source Inventory
artifacts.description: Inventory of all log sources feeding into the SIEM with coverage analysis

artifacts.name: SIEM Rule Tuning Records
artifacts.description: Records of SIEM rule tuning and use case updates

title: Endpoint Protection
verbatim: Financial institutions shall deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions on all endpoints including servers, workstations, and mobile devices. EDR solutions shall provide real-time threat detection, automated response capabilities, and centralised management. Signature updates shall occur at least daily.
translation: Deploy EDR on all endpoints — servers, workstations, and mobile devices. The EDR solution must provide real-time detection, automated response, and centralised management. Keep signatures updated at least daily.
evidence: Signature update schedule and compliance records

artifacts.name: EDR Deployment Documentation
artifacts.description: Documentation of EDR deployment coverage across all endpoints

artifacts.name: EDR Configuration Evidence
artifacts.description: Configuration and policy settings for EDR solution

artifacts.name: EDR Coverage and Compliance Report
artifacts.description: Report on EDR coverage, signature update compliance, and detection metrics

title: Email Security
verbatim: Financial institutions shall implement email security controls including spam filtering, malware scanning, phishing detection, and data loss prevention. Email authentication protocols such as SPF, DKIM, and DMARC shall be implemented for all organisational domains to prevent email spoofing.
translation: Implement comprehensive email security: spam filters, malware scanning, phishing detection, and DLP. Deploy SPF, DKIM, and DMARC on all your domains to prevent attackers from spoofing your email addresses.
evidence: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configuration records

artifacts.name: Email Security Configuration
artifacts.description: Configuration documentation for email security controls and filtering

artifacts.name: Email Authentication Records
artifacts.description: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configuration records for all organisational domains

artifacts.name: Email Security Metrics Report
artifacts.description: Report on email security metrics including phishing detection rates

title: DLP Controls
verbatim: Financial institutions shall implement data loss prevention controls to detect and prevent unauthorised transmission or exfiltration of sensitive data. DLP policies shall be aligned with the institution's data classification framework and shall cover email, web, endpoint, and cloud channels.
translation: Deploy DLP tools to detect and block unauthorised transfer of sensitive data. DLP policies must align with your data classification and cover all major channels: email, web browsing, endpoints, and cloud services.

artifacts.name: DLP Policy
artifacts.description: Policy defining data loss prevention requirements aligned with data classification

artifacts.name: DLP Configuration Evidence
artifacts.description: Configuration evidence for DLP controls across email, web, endpoint, and cloud

artifacts.name: DLP Incident Records
artifacts.description: Records of DLP alerts and incidents with investigation outcomes

title: Vulnerability Assessment
verbatim: Financial institutions shall conduct vulnerability assessments of all internet-facing systems at least monthly and internal systems at least quarterly. Assessments shall use industry-recognised tools and databases. Identified vulnerabilities shall be risk-rated, prioritised, and remediated within defined timeframes.
translation: Scan internet-facing systems for vulnerabilities at least monthly and internal systems at least quarterly. Use industry-standard tools, rate the severity of what you find, and fix vulnerabilities within defined timeframes based on their risk level.
evidence: Monthly scan results for internet-facing systems
Quarterly scan results for internal systems

artifacts.name: Vulnerability Assessment Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure for conducting regular vulnerability assessments with defined schedules

artifacts.name: Vulnerability Assessment Reports
artifacts.description: Monthly and quarterly vulnerability scan results with risk ratings

artifacts.name: Vulnerability Remediation Tracking
artifacts.description: Tracking log of vulnerability remediation against defined timeframes

title: Penetration Testing
verbatim: Financial institutions shall conduct penetration testing of critical systems and applications at least annually and prior to major releases. Testing shall be performed by qualified and independent testers. Findings shall be risk-rated, reported to management, and remediated within agreed timeframes.
translation: Conduct penetration testing on critical systems at least once a year and before major releases. Use qualified, independent testers. Risk-rate the findings, report them to management, and fix them within agreed timelines.
evidence: Penetration test reports from qualified testers

artifacts.name: Penetration Testing Policy
artifacts.description: Policy defining penetration testing scope, frequency, and qualification requirements

artifacts.name: Penetration Test Reports
artifacts.description: Annual penetration test reports from qualified independent testers

artifacts.name: Remediation Tracking Records
artifacts.description: Records of penetration test finding remediation against agreed timeframes

title: Red Team Exercises
verbatim: Financial institutions should conduct red team exercises to test the effectiveness of their security controls and incident response capabilities against realistic attack scenarios. Red team exercises should simulate advanced persistent threats and should be conducted by experienced professionals with appropriate rules of engagement.
translation: Consider conducting red team exercises that simulate real-world advanced attacks to test your defences and incident response. Use experienced red team professionals and define clear rules of engagement before starting.
evidence: Red team exercise plan and rules of engagement

artifacts.name: Red Team Rules of Engagement
artifacts.description: Documented rules of engagement for red team exercises

artifacts.name: Red Team Exercise Reports
artifacts.description: Reports from red team exercises with findings and recommendations

artifacts.name: Red Team Improvement Plan
artifacts.description: Improvement plan addressing findings from red team exercises

title: Vulnerability Remediation
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a vulnerability remediation process with defined service level targets based on risk severity. Critical vulnerabilities shall be remediated within seven days, high vulnerabilities within thirty days, and medium vulnerabilities within ninety days. Exceptions shall require documented risk acceptance.
translation: Define clear remediation timelines by severity: critical vulnerabilities within 7 days, high within 30 days, medium within 90 days. If you cannot meet these timelines, you need formal risk acceptance documentation.

artifacts.name: Vulnerability Remediation Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure defining remediation timelines and processes by risk severity

artifacts.name: Remediation Compliance Report
artifacts.description: Report on vulnerability remediation compliance against defined SLTs

artifacts.name: Remediation Exception Records
artifacts.description: Risk acceptance records for vulnerabilities not remediated within SLTs

title: Bug Bounty & Disclosure
verbatim: Financial institutions should consider establishing a coordinated vulnerability disclosure program or bug bounty program to leverage external security researchers in identifying vulnerabilities. Such programs should define clear scope, rules of engagement, and safe harbour provisions for participating researchers.
translation: Consider running a bug bounty or coordinated vulnerability disclosure program so external security researchers can report vulnerabilities they find. Define clear scope, rules, and safe harbour provisions to encourage responsible disclosure.
evidence: Vulnerability disclosure policy or bug bounty program documentation
Program scope and rules of engagement

artifacts.name: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
artifacts.description: Policy for coordinated vulnerability disclosure or bug bounty program

artifacts.name: Bug Bounty Program Rules
artifacts.description: Rules of engagement and scope for bug bounty participants

artifacts.name: Disclosure Program Records
artifacts.description: Records of reported vulnerabilities and remediation through the disclosure program

title: Threat Intelligence Program
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a cyber threat intelligence program to collect, analyse, and act upon threat intelligence relevant to the financial sector. The program shall incorporate multiple intelligence sources and shall produce actionable intelligence for security operations and risk management decisions.
translation: You need a formal threat intelligence program that gathers, analyses, and acts on cyber threat information relevant to banking. Use multiple intelligence sources and produce actionable intelligence that drives security operations and risk decisions.

artifacts.name: Threat Intelligence Program
artifacts.description: Documented threat intelligence program covering collection, analysis, and dissemination

artifacts.name: Threat Intelligence Reports
artifacts.description: Periodic threat intelligence reports with actionable findings

artifacts.name: Intelligence Source Inventory
artifacts.description: Inventory of threat intelligence sources used by the program

title: Threat Information Sharing
verbatim: Financial institutions shall participate in threat information sharing arrangements with industry peers, government agencies, and relevant information sharing and analysis centres. Shared information shall be handled in accordance with traffic light protocol or equivalent classification to protect source confidentiality.
translation: Participate in threat information sharing with industry peers, government agencies, and ISACs. Use the Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) or equivalent to handle shared information appropriately and protect the confidentiality of sources.
evidence: Records of information shared and received

artifacts.name: Threat Sharing Agreements
artifacts.description: Agreements and memberships for threat information sharing arrangements

artifacts.name: TLP Handling Procedures
artifacts.description: Procedures for handling shared threat intelligence per Traffic Light Protocol

artifacts.name: Information Sharing Records
artifacts.description: Records of threat information shared and received through sharing arrangements

title: Threat Hunting
verbatim: Financial institutions should establish proactive threat hunting capabilities to identify threats that may have evaded automated detection mechanisms. Threat hunting activities should be informed by threat intelligence and should follow structured hypotheses-driven or intelligence-driven methodologies.
translation: Go beyond automated detection by proactively hunting for threats that may have slipped through. Use structured methodologies driven by threat intelligence or specific hypotheses about potential attack vectors in your environment.

artifacts.name: Threat Hunting Program
artifacts.description: Documented threat hunting program with methodologies and playbooks

artifacts.name: Threat Hunting Reports
artifacts.description: Reports from threat hunting activities with findings and improvements

artifacts.name: Hunt-to-Detection Records
artifacts.description: Records of threat hunting findings converted to automated detection rules

title: IoC Management
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish processes for managing indicators of compromise (IoCs) including collection, validation, enrichment, and integration with security monitoring tools. IoCs shall be maintained in a threat intelligence platform and regularly updated to reflect the current threat landscape.
translation: Manage IoCs through a structured process: collect them, validate them, enrich them with context, and feed them into your security tools for automated detection. Keep IoCs current and maintained in a threat intelligence platform.

artifacts.name: IoC Management Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure for collecting, validating, enriching, and operationalising indicators of compromise

artifacts.name: TIP Configuration
artifacts.description: Configuration of threat intelligence platform for IoC management

artifacts.name: IoC Integration Evidence
artifacts.description: Evidence of IoC integration with security monitoring and detection tools

title: Cyber Situational Awareness
verbatim: Financial institutions shall maintain cyber situational awareness through continuous monitoring of the external threat landscape, the institution's attack surface, and emerging threats targeting the financial sector. Situational awareness reports shall be provided to senior management and security operations on a regular basis.
translation: Stay aware of the cyber threat landscape by continuously monitoring external threats, your own attack surface, and emerging threats to the financial sector. Provide regular situational awareness reports to senior management and the security team.

artifacts.name: Situational Awareness Program
artifacts.description: Program for maintaining cyber situational awareness and attack surface monitoring

artifacts.name: Situational Awareness Reports
artifacts.description: Regular reports on cyber threat landscape and institutional exposure

artifacts.name: Attack Surface Monitoring
artifacts.description: Evidence of continuous monitoring of the institution's external attack surface
